folders.baseDirHint: Organized files will be moved here.
folders.title: Watched folders
folders.hint: New files in these folders are automatically analyzed.

rules.title: Rules
rules.hint: Mapping of document types to target folders and filenames.
rules.name: Name:

wizard.title: New Rule
wizard.step1hint: Choose the category for documents this rule will match. When a file is classified, it will be tagged with this type.
wizard.step2hint: Define the matching method. Keywords mode checks if the document text contains specific words. Regex mode uses a pattern for advanced matching.
wizard.step2simpleHint: Enter words that typically appear in this type of document. The more keywords, the more accurate the match. A file is classified when the minimum number of keywords is found.
wizard.step2regexHint: Enter a regular expression without delimiters. The pattern is case-insensitive. Example: invoice.*total.*\d+
wizard.step3hint: Set the destination folder and filename pattern. Use {YYYY} in the folder path for automatic year-based sorting.
wizard.step3folderHint: Folder path. Use {YYYY} for year substitution. Example: Finance/Invoices/{YYYY}/
wizard.step3nameHint: Filename template. Available placeholders: {Sender}, {Date}. Example: Invoice_{Sender}_{Date}
wizard.step4hint: Check the rule settings before creating. You can always edit or disable it later.

onboarding.intro: Ablage helps you organize documents automatically. Here's how to get started:
onboarding.step1: Choose a folder where organized files will be moved. Ablage will create subfolders like Finance/Invoices/2026/ automatically.
onboarding.step2: Add folders to monitor (e.g. Downloads, Desktop). New files in these folders will be analyzed.
onboarding.step3: Copy or save a PDF, DOCX, or image into a watched folder. Within a few seconds you'll see a suggestion notification.
onboarding.step4: Review the suggestion: the detected type, proposed name, and target folder. Click Apply to move the file, or Skip to ignore.
onboarding.showAgain: You can reopen this guide from the About tab.

help.title: How it works
help.tip1: Ablage runs in the system tray. Close the window — it keeps working in the background.
help.tip2: Rules determine how files are classified. Each rule has keywords or a regex pattern that is matched against the file content.
help.tip3: Simple rules use comma-separated keywords. A file matches when enough keywords are found (configurable threshold).
help.tip4: Regex rules use a regular expression. The file matches if the pattern is found anywhere in the text.
help.tip5: You can undo any move from the History tab. The file will be returned to its original location.
help.tip6: Use {Sender}, {Date}, {YYYY} placeholders in name templates. Example: Invoice_{Sender}_{Date}

guide.label: Documentation
guide.title: User Guide
guide.step1text: Go to the Folders tab and choose a target folder. This is the root directory where Ablage will create subfolders and move organized files.
guide.step2text: Add one or more folders to monitor. Ablage watches these for new PDF, DOCX, JPG, and PNG files. Typical choices are Downloads or Desktop.
guide.step3text: Save or copy a document into a watched folder. Within seconds, a notification appears with the detected type, a suggested filename, and the target folder.
guide.step4text: Click Apply to rename and move the file automatically. Click Skip to ignore. Every action can be undone from the History tab.
guide.rulesIntro: Rules determine how documents are classified. Each rule has a pattern that is matched against the extracted text of a file. When a match is found, the file is tagged with the rule's document type.
guide.keywordsText: Enter comma-separated words that commonly appear in this type of document. A file is classified when at least the minimum number of keywords is found in the text.
guide.keywordsExample: A file containing "invoice number" and "total amount" matches with 2 hits.
guide.regexText: For advanced patterns, use a regular expression. The pattern is tested case-insensitively against the full document text. A single match is enough to classify.
guide.regexExample: Matches text like "Account Statement ... Debit ... Credit".
guide.templatesIntro: Templates control how files are renamed. Use placeholders that get replaced with values extracted from the document.
guide.tmplSender: Company or sender name detected in the document
guide.tmplYYYY: Year, used in folder paths for automatic sorting
guide.undoText: Every move operation is logged. Open the History tab to see all past actions. Hover over a completed entry and click Undo to return the file to its original location with its original name.
guide.trayText: Ablage runs in the system tray. You can close the settings window — monitoring continues in the background. Right-click the tray icon to see watched folders, pause monitoring, or quit the app.
